subject: Re: BoxNet picking issues with UltraAuFoil grids
body:
I think that is best answered by Dimitry. We haven't had much luck playing 
with the CTF-estimation parameters for Ultraufoils and we don't get the 
same problems with grids where the foil is made from lighter metals and 
alloys.

On Friday, September 23, 2022 at 4:43:45 PM UTC+3 mjja...@gmail.com wrote:

> Thanks for the response Martin.
>
> Do you thing this is due to the BoxNet picker or the CTF estimation? The 
> contrast of micrographs with significant gold looks off, which suggests to 
> me that there is a CTF estimation issue.
>
> Thanks,
> Matt
>
> On Fri, Sep 23, 2022, 12:04 AM Martin Hallberg <b.m.ha...@gmail.com> 
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Matt,
>> This is common with Warp BoxNet particle picking. We try to avoid to have 
>> the gold in the image but if we anyway end up with it on a part of the data 
>> we pick using other tools. If needed for further processing in the Warp/M 
>> suite, one can always import particles (or just positions) to Warp usually 
>> then after some 2D or 3D "cleaning". I would love to hear if someone has a 
>> solution!
>>
>> Best,
>>
>> Martin
>>
>>
>>
>> On Thursday, September 22, 2022 at 8:00:39 PM UTC+3 mjja...@gmail.com 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hello,
>>>
>>> I'm having BoxNet picking issues when there is a considerable amount of 
>>> the UltraAuFoil grid scaffold in the images:
>>>
>>> BoxNet seems to be missing a lot of particles. This doesn’t happen when 
>>> less gold is in the image. Any way to fix this?
>>>
>>> Here are my CTF settings:
>>>
>>>
>>> Thank you,
>>>
>>> Matt
>>>
>>> --
